text: If you had to eat one cuisine for the next month — Italian, Thai, Mexican, Japanese, or Indian — which one survives and which one dies first?
Build a perfect sandwich: pick the bread, three fillings, and one ingredient that shouldn't work but somehow does.
If your phone could only keep five apps after tonight, which five survive?
Which extinct animal would you keep as a pocket-sized pet — and what would you name it?
Which is the worse house guest: the one who never helps clean up, or the one who silently reorganizes your kitchen?
Name a smell that drops you straight into a specific memory from before you were 12.
Pick a song that loops in your head and explain what specifically about it sticks.
Name a piece of clothing in your closet you wear far more than you reasonably should.
If a dog wrote a one-star review of you as an owner, what would it say?
Describe your ideal vacation with an unlimited budget: where, how long, and who's coming with you?
What's the weirdest food combination you secretly love, and what made you try it the first time?
What would be the most useless superpower in everyday life — and what's the one situation it would actually save you?
Describe the last time you laughed so hard you cried — what was actually said in the moment?
What's the most embarrassing thing you've done on a first date — be specific about where you were and roughly when?
If animals could give TED talks, which species would have the most boring topic and what would it be?
Pick one chore you'd happily outsource forever and one you actually enjoy doing.
If you swapped jobs with a specific celebrity for one week, who and what would you actually do on Monday morning?
Describe the worst birthday you remember — who was there, what went wrong, and how it ended.
Design a 30-minute commute you'd actually look forward to: pick the vehicle, the route, and what's playing in your ears.
If every adult had to take one mandatory week-long class each year, what should it cover?
Pick a movie ending you'd rewrite — name the movie and what happens instead.
Walk us through your last impulse purchase over $50 — what was it, and would you do it again knowing what you know now?
Name three small habits you'd want a kid you raised to grow up with.
Which historical event would you most want to witness for 24 hours, and from which exact spot in the room?
Pick a city you've never been to that you've quietly built up in your head — what's it like in your imagination?
If you had to give a 15-minute TEDx talk next month, what's the title and one idea you'd argue for?
What's a moment a stranger was unexpectedly kind to you that you still remember years later?
If you could re-do your high school graduation day exactly once, what specifically would you change?
If you DJ'd a friend's wedding tomorrow, what's the first song you'd play and what's the very last?
Pitch a reality TV show concept that you'd genuinely sit through ten episodes of.
Name one rule in your country or culture you quietly think is stupid — and why it persists anyway.
If you could ban one specific product (not a category — an exact product) from existing, which one and why?
Pick a fictional world you'd actually want to live in for a full year — and one you'd visit for only an afternoon.
Design a restaurant: pick the theme, the signature dish, and one weird policy customers have to follow.
If you had a time machine that only went back 10 minutes, how would you actually use it this week?
If you had to spend a year in either a small mountain village or a coastal town of 200 people, which and why?
Pick a generation other than yours and one thing you genuinely admire about how they do things.
Pick a friendship in your life that faded — what was the actual conversation or month it ended?
If you were running a country of 10 million people tomorrow, what's the first law you'd pass on day one?
When you were 16, what version of yourself did you expect to be by now — what matches and what doesn't?
What's an opinion you used to defend hard in arguments that you now privately disagree with?
If you could send a 30-second video to your 13-year-old self, what's in it?
Pick one thing about how humans live today that you think people 100 years from now will find barbaric.
Invent a new public holiday — name it, pick the date, and one thing everyone has to do on it.
Describe a compliment that made you genuinely uncomfortable, and explain why it hit weird.
Design a new religion with exactly three rules — what are they, and which one would you break first?
What's advice you've given more than once that you yourself don't actually follow?
Tell us about a time you completely changed your mind about a person — what was the specific moment?
If you could ask one specific dead person one specific question, who and what would you ask?
Pitch a new sport that fuses two existing ones — name it, give three rules, and one weird piece of equipment.
Name a memory from this past year that you already know you'll still remember in ten years.
